questions.question: Which aggregate function returns the total number of rows in a result set?
options: SUM()
COUNT()
TOTAL()
ROWS()
questions.answer: COUNT()

questions.question: What does SELECT SUM(credits) FROM courses; return?
options: The average of the credits column
The total of all values in the credits column
The number of rows in the courses table
The maximum value in the credits column
questions.answer: The total of all values in the credits column

questions.question: Which aggregate function calculates the arithmetic mean of a numeric column?
options: MEAN()
AVERAGE()
AVG()
MID()
questions.answer: AVG()

questions.question: What is the purpose of the GROUP BY clause?
options: To sort results in ascending order
To split rows into groups and apply aggregate functions to each group
To filter individual rows before aggregation
To limit the number of rows returned
questions.answer: To split rows into groups and apply aggregate functions to each group

questions.question: Which clause filters groups after aggregation?
options: WHERE
HAVING
GROUP BY
ORDER BY
questions.answer: HAVING

questions.question: What does COUNT(*) count?
options: Only non-NULL values in a specific column
All rows including those with NULL values
Only distinct values
Only numeric columns
questions.answer: All rows including those with NULL values

questions.question: Which SQL statement is invalid?
options: SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students GROUP BY major;
SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students WHERE COUNT(*) > 2 GROUP BY major;
SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students GROUP BY major HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM students;
questions.answer: SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students WHERE COUNT(*) > 2 GROUP BY major;

questions.question: When does the WHERE clause filter rows relative to GROUP BY?
options: After GROUP BY
Before GROUP BY
At the same time as GROUP BY
WHERE cannot be used with GROUP BY
questions.answer: Before GROUP BY

questions.question: When does the HAVING clause filter groups relative to GROUP BY?
options: Before GROUP BY
After GROUP BY
HAVING replaces GROUP BY
At the same time as WHERE
questions.answer: After GROUP BY

questions.question: Which query correctly finds the minimum and maximum age of students?
options: SELECT MINIMUM(age), MAXIMUM(age) FROM students;
SELECT MIN(age), MAX(age) FROM students;
SELECT LEAST(age), GREATEST(age) FROM students;
SELECT LOW(age), HIGH(age) FROM students;
questions.answer: SELECT MIN(age), MAX(age) FROM students;

questions.question: What happens if you use an aggregate function in a WHERE clause?
options: It works correctly
It returns NULL
It causes an error
It is silently ignored
questions.answer: It causes an error

questions.question: Which query correctly counts the number of students in each major?
options: SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students;
SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students GROUP BY major;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM students GROUP BY major;
SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students HAVING major;
questions.answer: SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students GROUP BY major;

questions.question: What does the ROUND() function do when used with AVG()?
options: It rounds the result to the nearest integer by default
It truncates the result
It rounds the average to a specified number of decimal places
It converts the result to a string
questions.answer: It rounds the average to a specified number of decimal places

questions.question: Which query shows only majors with more than 2 students?
options: SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students WHERE COUNT(*) > 2 GROUP BY major;
SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students GROUP BY major HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;
SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students GROUP BY major WHERE COUNT(*) > 2;
SELECT major FROM students HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;
questions.answer: SELECT major, COUNT(*) FROM students GROUP BY major HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;

questions.question: Can you use WHERE and HAVING in the same query?
options: No, they are mutually exclusive
Yes, WHERE filters rows before grouping and HAVING filters groups after
Yes, but they do the same thing
Only if GROUP BY is not used
questions.answer: Yes, WHERE filters rows before grouping and HAVING filters groups after

questions.question: What does SELECT AVG(gpa) FROM students; return if the table has no rows?
options: NULL
An error
An empty string
questions.answer: NULL

questions.question: Which query groups courses by both department and credits?
options: SELECT department, credits, COUNT(*) FROM courses GROUP BY department AND credits;
SELECT department, credits, COUNT(*) FROM courses GROUP BY department, credits;
SELECT department, credits, COUNT(*) FROM courses GROUP BY (department, credits);
SELECT department + credits, COUNT(*) FROM courses GROUP BY department;
questions.answer: SELECT department, credits, COUNT(*) FROM courses GROUP BY department, credits;

questions.question: What is an alias in the context of aggregate queries?
options: A backup name for a table
A temporary name given to a column or expression using AS
A type of JOIN operation
A way to rename a database
questions.answer: A temporary name given to a column or expression using AS

questions.question: Which clause would you use to sort the results of a GROUP BY query?
options: SORT BY
ARRANGE BY
ORDER BY
RANK BY
questions.answer: ORDER BY

questions.question: What does LIMIT 1 do at the end of a query?
options: Returns only the first row of the result set
Limits the query to one column
Limits the number of groups to one
Filters rows where the count equals one
questions.answer: Returns only the first row of the result set

questions.question: Which aggregate function would you NOT use on a text column?
options: COUNT()
MIN()
MAX()
SUM()
questions.answer: SUM()

questions.question: In SELECT major, AVG(gpa) FROM students GROUP BY major HAVING AVG(gpa) > 3.0; what does HAVING filter on?
options: Individual student GPAs
The average GPA of each major group
The total GPA sum
Students with GPA above 3.0
questions.answer: The average GPA of each major group

questions.question: What is the correct order of clauses in a SQL query that uses WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, and ORDER BY?
options: GROUP BY, WHERE, HAVING, ORDER BY
WHERE, HAVING, GROUP BY, ORDER BY
WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, ORDER BY
HAVING, WHERE, GROUP BY, ORDER BY
questions.answer: WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, ORDER BY

questions.question: How does COUNT(column_name) differ from COUNT(*)?
options: COUNT(column_name) counts only non-NULL values in that column
There is no difference
COUNT(column_name) is faster
COUNT(*) counts only non-NULL values
questions.answer: COUNT(column_name) counts only non-NULL values in that column

questions.question: Which query finds majors where the average GPA of students aged 21 or older is above 3.2?
options: SELECT major, AVG(gpa) FROM students HAVING age >= 21 AND AVG(gpa) > 3.2 GROUP BY major;
SELECT major, AVG(gpa) FROM students WHERE age >= 21 GROUP BY major HAVING AVG(gpa) > 3.2;
SELECT major, AVG(gpa) FROM students GROUP BY major WHERE age >= 21 HAVING AVG(gpa) > 3.2;
SELECT major, AVG(gpa) FROM students WHERE AVG(gpa) > 3.2 GROUP BY major HAVING age >= 21;
questions.answer: SELECT major, AVG(gpa) FROM students WHERE age >= 21 GROUP BY major HAVING AVG(gpa) > 3.2;
